sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 16.13 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.16)

statement.latex: Does there exist a finite$p$-group$G$all of whose maximal subgroups$H$are special, that is, satisfy$Z(H)=[H,H]= (H)$? { Ya.\,G.\,Berkovich} Yes, for all primes there are groups of arbitrarily large size with this property (J.\,Cossey, Bull. Austral. Math. Soc., { 89} (2014), 415--419); an example for$p=2$given by a Sylow$2$-subgroup of$L_3(4)$was independently presented by V.\,I.\,Zenkov at Mal'ce...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
